clinical_study.brief_title: Olaparib Plus Docetaxel vs. Docetaxel Alone in BRCA1/2-Mutated Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (OLADO-mCRPC)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Olaparib in Combination With Docetaxel Versus Docetaxel Alone in Patients With BRCA1 or BRCA2-Mutated Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Who Have Progressed on Next-Generation Androgen Receptor-Targeted Therapy

brief_summary.textblock: This trial tests whether adding olaparib (a PARP inhibitor) to docetaxel chemotherapy improves radiographic progression-free survival compared to docetaxel alone in patients with BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have progressed on next-generation androgen receptor pathway inhibitors (e.g., enzalutamide, abiraterone, apalutamide) and who are candidates for docetaxel chemotherapy.

detailed_description.textblock: mCRPC harboring BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutations (germline or somatic) is exquisitely sensitive to PARP inhibition due to homologous recombination repair deficiency. Olaparib monotherapy (PROfound trial) demonstrated superiority over enzalutamide/abiraterone in BRCA1/2-mutated mCRPC. In patients with AR splice variant expression (e.g., AR-V7), resistance to androgen-receptor-targeted therapies is well-established, making chemotherapy a rational next step. Preclinical data suggest synergy between PARP inhibition and taxane-based chemotherapy. This study randomizes BRCA1/2-mutated mCRPC patients who have progressed on an AR-targeted agent to docetaxel 75 mg/m2 IV Q3W + prednisone 5 mg BID + olaparib 300 mg BID (tablets, continuous) versus docetaxel 75 mg/m2 IV Q3W + prednisone 5 mg BID alone, for up to 10 cycles of docetaxel followed by olaparib maintenance (experimental arm) or observation (control arm). AR-V7 status is collected as a stratification factor and exploratory biomarker. All patients must maintain ongoing androgen deprivation therapy (surgical or medical castration, testosterone < 50 ng/dL).

primary_outcome.measure: Radiographic Progression-Free Survival (rPFS) by PCWG3-modified RECIST 1.1
primary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until radiographic progression or death, assessed up to 18 months

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until death from any cause, up to 36 months

secondary_outcome.measure: PSA50 Response Rate (>= 50% decline from baseline PSA)
secondary_outcome.time_frame: At 12 weeks and best response, up to 18 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Objective Response Rate (ORR) in patients with measurable soft tissue disease

secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until first SSE, up to 36 months
During treatment period, up to 18 months

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed prostate adenocarcinoma - Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC): documented metastatic disease on imaging (bone scan and/or CT/MRI) with PSA progression (2 consecutive rises at least 1 week apart) or radiographic progression while maintaining castrate testosterone levels (< 50 ng/dL) - BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation confirmed by a validated assay (germline or somatic); testing must be performed on tumor tissue or germline DNA; other HRR mutations (ATM, CDK12, PALB2, etc.) are not eligible for primary analysis (may be enrolled in exploratory cohort) - Documented progression on at least one next-generation androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (enzalutamide, abiraterone acetate, apalutamide, or darolutamide) given for mCRPC or nmCRPC - Candidate for docetaxel chemotherapy per treating physician assessment - Ongoing androgen deprivation therapy (surgical or medical castration); testosterone < 50 ng/dL within 28 days of enrollment - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-2 - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1500/mm3, platelets >= 100,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 9 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 1.5x ULN - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 51 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault) - No prior cytotoxic chemotherapy for mCRPC (docetaxel given for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer allowed if completed > 12 months prior) - AR-V7 status collection by validated assay (circulating tumor cells or tumor tissue) strongly encouraged; results do not affect eligibility - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Prior PARP inhibitor therapy (olaparib, rucaparib, niraparib, talazoparib) - Prior docetaxel for mCRPC (docetaxel for mHSPC completed > 12 months prior is allowed) - Pure small cell or neuroendocrine prostate cancer (mixed adenocarcinoma with neuroendocrine features allowed) - Active or symptomatic CNS metastases - ECOG Performance Status >= 3 - Known myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) or active hematologic malignancy - Prior allogeneic stem cell or bone marrow transplantation - Peripheral neuropathy >= Grade 2 at baseline - Active major infection or fever (> 38.5 C) within 7 days of enrollment - Concurrent treatment with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers that cannot be discontinued (pharmacokinetic interaction with olaparib) - Known hypersensitivity to olaparib, docetaxel, or prednisone - History of active second malignancy within 3 years (adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer or bladder cancer in situ allowed)

intervention.description: Olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily continuously, plus docetaxel 75 mg/m2 IV every 3 weeks for up to 10 cycles, plus prednisone 5 mg orally twice daily. Following completion of docetaxel, patients continue olaparib 300 mg BID as maintenance until progression or unacceptable toxicity.
Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 IV every 3 weeks for up to 10 cycles, plus prednisone 5 mg orally twice daily. Following completion of docetaxel, observation until progression.
